name: Helen Merrill
abstract: Helen Merrill (born Jelena Ana Milcetic; July 21, 1929) is an American jazz vocalist. Her first album, the eponymous 1954 recording Helen Merrill (with Clifford Brown on EmArcy), was an immediate success and associated her with the first generation of bebop jazz musicians.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Jazz Concert presented by The Assorted Nuts
Body and Soul: The Music of Ella Fitzgerald

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Katz
about_quotes.evidence: Helen and I wanted to do this record... I wrote these charts and I hired Thad Jones... we made these tapes and they came out great... that was one of the first records we put out.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Katz interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: Helen Merrill. Wonderful singer. ... Made a nice duo record with her, a wonderful record.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: Dick Katz did some very interesting arrangements for Helen Merrill... That made me want to play that tune more than hearing Ornette play it
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jonah Jones
about_quotes.evidence: he would get us singing together... he'd have Eddie, me and Benny Green over here... and then we'd be singing... Helen Merrill over here

about_quotes.speaker: Jake Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: They’ve got Helen Merrill and her little quartet... Terry Clarke, an old pal of mine, he’s going to be playing drums with them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jake Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Schaap
about_quotes.evidence: I don’t know whether he had started his association with Helen Merrill at that time but if he had I would have known about it
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Schaap interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003
